description: Data Integrity Risk: Detect silent corruption, mapping errors, and partial-write hazards.

fixtures.notes: Down migration restores only the dropped column shape, not the historical values, so rollback silently loses existing provider_group data.
fixtures.runtime_state_condition: Rows already contain non-null provider_group values before Up, and operators later execute Down during rollback.

fixtures.notes: dump_all dedupes work by schema file path, so a later database config can be skipped even when it has divergent schema state that still needs to be dumped.
fixtures.runtime_state_condition: Multiple database configs in the same environment resolve to the same schema_path while their underlying database states differ.

fixtures.notes: Removing per-battle locking allows concurrent handlers to interleave updates for the same battle_tag, risking state corruption and lost transitions.
fixtures.runtime_state_condition: Two or more websocket messages for the same battle_tag are processed concurrently.

fixtures.notes: Per-battle lock initialization and guarded handling are added back, serializing updates and preserving state integrity under concurrent message delivery.
fixtures.runtime_state_condition: Concurrent updates target the same battle_tag, but the lock ensures only one handler mutates shared state at a time.

fixtures.notes: The migration is made explicitly irreversible on down, which fails fast instead of pretending dropped relational data can be recreated safely.
fixtures.runtime_state_condition: A destructive migration has already run and an operator attempts rollback later.
